text.title: nodejs-patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for nodejs-patterns as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Placing error handler middleware before routes. Error handlers must be last in the middleware chain to catch all errors.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Errors thrown in routes never reach an error handler placed earlier. The request hangs or Express sends a generic response.

failure-mode.description: Duplicating validation logic in controllers instead of using a shared validate middleware or asyncHandler wrapper.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Validation boilerplate spreads across controllers, diverging error handling and making it hard to enforce consistent error shapes.

failure-mode.description: Importing process.env variables throughout the codebase instead of resolving once in config/env.ts.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Scattered process.env accesses are hard to test, type, and refactor. Configuration changes require searching the entire codebase.

failure-mode.description: Not creating a pure createApp() function. Binding listen() inside createApp() prevents testing without binding a real port.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Tests must bind a port for each test file, slowing test suites and risking port conflicts. Separating app factory from server startup enables fast, deterministic tests.
